items.claim: BSD is physically larger than his opponents in the lightweight division.
items.quote: BSD is enormous for this division

items.claim: Patty Pimblett performs poorly when fighting on the back foot.
items.quote: Patty doesn't do so good on the back foot.

items.claim: Patty Pimblett's striking defense is very poor.
items.quote: His defense is very, very bad.

items.claim: Patty Pimblett lacks the technical striking ability to generate power while moving backward.
items.quote: Patty's not as good of a striker to do it.

items.claim: Patty Pimblett has poor boxing mechanics, specifically regarding head movement and hand positioning.
items.quote: He wings his shots, chin up in the air, doesn't move his head, doesn't pick up his hands that much.

items.claim: Benoit Saint Denis has a stylistic advantage as a southpaw against Patty Pimblett.
items.quote: BSD is a southpaw, so he's gonna have that advantage as well because Patty doesn't fight a lot of southpaws.

items.claim: Patty Pimblett possesses better cardio than Benoit Saint Denis.
items.quote: I believe he has better cardio.

items.claim: Benoit Saint Denis may be physically compromised due to a recurring leg injury.
items.quote: He's got another crater on his leg this time. So, he might be compromised

items.claim: Conor McGregor has been inactive, fighting only four times in the last eight years.
items.quote: four fights in the last eight years is not great.

items.claim: Max Holloway is moving up two weight classes to fight at 170 pounds.
items.quote: it's two divisions above where he used to fight.

items.claim: Max Holloway's chin has declined, as evidenced by him being hurt or dropped in his last four fights.
items.quote: Max's chin is not what it used to be. He's gotten hurt in his last four fights.

items.claim: Conor McGregor's fighting style is effective at disrupting Max Holloway's rhythmic striking.
items.quote: He's got a very disruptive style. Breaks momentum, breaks rhythm constantly with every single shot.
